title: hypericum galioides var. reductum
intro: Hypericum galioides var. reductum, also known as the small St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the St. John's Wort family, Hypericaceae. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant's extracts have been shown to exhibit potential health benefits, including antioxidant and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. Hypericum galioides var. reductum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
